items.title: Lagarde Says Inflation, Growth Risks Now More Broadly Balanced
items.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said risks to euro-area inflation and growth have become less pronounced.

items.title: Nissan CEO Says It Still Makes Sense to Build Cars in US
items.description: Nissan President and CEO Ivan Espinosa says it still makes sense to build cars in the US, despite trade tensions and a weaker yen. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US Stocks Decline as Traders Await Warsh Comments, Fresh Data
items.description: US stocks declined before the opening bell on Wednesday as traders awaited unscripted commentary from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh and fresh economic data.

items.title: US Companies Add 98,000 Jobs in June, ADP Says
items.description: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June after a 122,000 increase in the prior month, according to ADP Research data out Wednesday. The median estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists called for a

items.title: Fertilizer Firm Draws $12 Billion In Orders in Oman IPO
items.description: Oman India Fertiliser Co.’s initial public offering has drawn over $12 billion in orders, in the Middle East’s largest listing since the outbreak of the regional conflict.

items.title: S&P Winning Streak for July at Risk With Volatile End to Month
items.description: July is a great time to buy stocks. In fact, it’s been the best month for the S&P 500 Index in the past two decades. Bulls are finding comfort in that history ahead of what stands to be an eventful st

items.title: Software Gains as Guggenheim Says AI No ‘Death Knell’ for Sector
items.description: Software shares rose in premarket trading after Guggenheim upgraded a trio of companies, saying fears that artificial intelligence poses a mortal threat to the sector are overdone.

items.title: Coffee, Sugar Extend Rally as Adverse Weather Fuels Supply Fears
items.description: Coffee and sugar futures extended gains in New York and London as unfavorable weather in key growing regions heightened supply concerns.

items.title: How Karlie Kloss Went From Cover Girl to Media Mogul
items.description: Emily Chang goes inside the world of supermodel-turned-entrepreneur Karlie Kloss to explore how she’s trying to revive magazine publishing. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Goldman Sachs Says Capital Spending Is Powering the Bull Market
items.description: The best quarter for stocks in six years ended on a high note, with chipmakers extending their rebound from war-driven lows and signs of economic resilience boosting confidence in corporate earnings.

items.title: Commodities Broker Pulled Back Into Tax Dividend Fraud Suit by UK Court
items.description: ED&F Man Capital Markets Ltd., now MCML Ltd., has been pulled back into a long-running fraud case linked to the Cum-Ex tax dividend scandal after the UK Supreme Court ruled that the Danish tax authori

items.title: Trump’s White House Takes Aim at EU Emissions Law
items.description: With the US warning it could ship its energy elsewhere, some countries in Europe are urging the bloc to revisit the rules.

items.title: US Dollar Climbs Ahead of Warsh; Anthropic Restrictions Lifted | Bloomberg Brief 7/1/2026
items.description: The US dollar strengthens as traders await Fed Chair Warsh's comments in his first public appearance alongside other central bank leaders in Sintra, Portugal. Oil declines as the US says indirect talk

items.title: Emerging Market Currencies Erase 2026 Gains as Dollar Advances
items.description: Emerging-market currencies erased gains for this year, with speculation over higher US interest rates sparking a resurgence in the US dollar.

items.title: JPMorgan Is Leading Race for French Cooling Network Provider Idex
items.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s asset management unit has emerged as the leading bidder to buy Antin Infrastructure Partners SA’s French heating and cooling network operator, Idex, people familiar with the mat

items.title: Where To Invest $100,000
items.description: If you had a sudden six-figure windfall, where should you invest it? Bloomberg asked top wealth advisers where they would put $100,000 (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: AI’s Trillion-Dollar Debt Binge Fuels Century-Old Private Market
items.description: A private bond market dating back more than a century is opening a new front in the trillion-dollar AI funding boom, allowing tech borrowers to sell debt directly to deep-pocketed insurance firms.

items.title: Kevin Warsh to Make Global Debut, US Lifts Anthropic Restrictions | The Pulse 7/1/2026
items.description: "The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story

items.title: Wayve Files for Share Sale on LSE’s New Private Market
items.description: Autonomous driving software company Wayve Technologies Ltd. has filed to sell shares on the London Stock Exchange’s new Private Securities Market, the first major company to test the novel system.

items.title: Kevin Warsh to Speak at Sintra, Trump's $1.4B in Crypto Earnings | The Opening Trade 7/1/2026
